reason: All three findings are minor structural issues with no information loss. The knowledge file contains all source content; findings relate only to consolidation depth and two missing hint terms for niche planning-status queries. No finding rises to medium impact.

findings_assessment.justification: All content from the source 構成 section is present in s4: interface/class definitions, processing flow description, and table definition with column details. The section is retrievable via comprehensive hints covering key class names and テーブル定義. Splitting into subsections would improve structure but would not change what information an agent can retrieve. Why it persisted: Phase E returned the file unchanged — the fix agent apparently judged that consolidation did not lose content and that restructuring a minor-severity finding was not warranted.

All configuration content is present in s5: the XML example, initialization requirement, and both property tables (BasicServiceAvailability and ServiceAvailabilityCheckHandler) with required/default/description columns. Hints are comprehensive and cover all key property names. A user asking any configuration question will retrieve s5 and find the needed detail. Why it persisted: Same as s4 — Phase E output is identical to Phase D input, indicating the fix agent left the file unchanged for minor structural findings where content is fully preserved.

'未検討' and '取り下げ' are internal planning-status terms, not terms end users typically search by. Users asking about feature availability would use feature-name queries (指定時間切り替え, 画面項目表示切り替え, etc.) or broader terms like 開閉局要求 or 未実装機能, all of which are already in s3's hints. The content for both subsections is fully present in s3. The risk of a user failing to find s3 due to these two missing hints is very low in practice. Why it persisted: Phase E returned the file unchanged; the fix was not applied despite being a straightforward hint addition. The fix agent likely deprioritized low-discoverability-impact hint additions.
